paragraphs.en: “Death of the Soccer God,” “Nebraska,” “Muskism,” and “Spawning Season.”

Death of the Soccer God, by Dimitry Elias Léger (MCD/FSG). This stylish historical picaresque examines Haitian identity and history by recounting the “brief, wondrous life” of a fictional athlete, Gilbert Chevalier. Born to a wealthy family in Port-au-Prince, the young Chevalier has a prodigious talent for soccer. When he’s dispatched to Columbia University for college, he rooms with Miles Davis, consorts with John Coltrane, and is recruited to play for the United States in the 1950 World Cup, in Brazil. There he meets a nine-year-old Pelé and leads the American team to a stunning upset of England. Returning to Haiti after playing professional football in Europe, Chevalier runs afoul of President François Duvalier—an incident with dangerous consequences.
paragraphs.zh: 《Death of the Soccer God》，作者Dimitry Elias Léger（MCD/FSG）。这部风格独到的历史体裁冒险小说，以虚构运动员吉尔伯特·谢瓦利耶（Gilbert Chevalier）“短暂而奇妙的一生”为线索，审视海地的身份与历史。年少成名于太子港的谢瓦利耶天赋异禀，擅长足球。赴哥伦比亚大学就读时，他与迈尔斯·戴维斯同住一室，与约翰·柯川有往来，并在1950年巴西世界杯中被征召为美国队出场。在那里他遇见了9岁的贝利，并率领美国队完成对英格兰的一次惊人逆转。退役后在欧洲踢职业足球，再回到海地的谢瓦利耶，与弗朗索瓦·杜瓦利埃总统发生冲突，由此招致危险后果。

paragraphs.en: Nebraska, by Monica Datta (Astra). This ambitious novel springs from a moment of apparent inhuman cruelty: a mother, Anna Chatterjee, kills her disabled son, pushing his wheelchair in front of an oncoming train. The murder and its fraught causes are recounted by a Bengali analyst, who is himself interpreting them from the records of a different analyst with connections to Chatterjee’s two living children. When these two children, along with Chatterjee’s husband, attempt to pick her up at the end of her prison sentence, they find she is already gone. Datta explores the impact of enormous colonial forces on the unconscious of a single individual, her narrative shifting between time lines and continents in a performance that is both dizzying and energizing.
paragraphs.zh: 《Nebraska》，作者Monica Datta（Astra）。这部雄心勃勃的小说源于一次几近非人的残忍行为：母亲安娜·查特吉（Anna Chatterjee）杀死了她的残障儿子，将他推上轮椅推向迎面驶来的列车。谋杀及其复杂原因由一位孟加拉裔分析师叙述，这位分析师又是依据另一位分析师的档案来解读这些记录；而那位分析师与查特吉的两个在世子女有联系。随着这两个孩子与查特吉的丈夫在她刑期结束后去接她时，才发现她已经不在世。达塔（Datta）探讨了巨大的殖民力量对单个人无意识深层的冲击，叙事在时间线与洲际之间来回切换，以一种令人眩晕又振奋人心的方式展开。

paragraphs.en: Discover notable new fiction, nonfiction, and poetry.
paragraphs.zh: 发现值得关注的新小说、非虚构文学与诗歌。

paragraphs.en: Muskism, by Quinn Slobodian and Ben Tarnoff (Harper). What is the philosophy that underpins Elon Musk’s varied ventures, from Tesla to SpaceX to DOGE? The answer, this slim study contends, is a creed in which “states and individuals” can achieve “sovereignty through technology.” The portrait of Musk that emerges here is that of a childish, terminally online man who desires to eliminate the messiness of contemporary life for a more rigid order. “To say that Muskism is worth taking seriously is not to say that its success is guaranteed,” Slobodian and Tarnoff write. But the “institutional breakdown” of the twenty-first century, they warn, could mean that Muskism will profoundly shape the world to come.
paragraphs.zh: 《Muskism》（作者：Quinn Slobodian 与 Ben Tarnoff，Harper 出版社）。埃隆·马斯克从特斯拉、SpaceX 到 DOGE 的多样化事业，其背后是什么哲学？这部篇幅不长的研究给出的答案是：一种信念，即“国家与个人”可以“通过技术实现主权”。在这本书中浮现出的马斯克形象，是一个童稚、沉迷网络的“末日终端网民”式人物，渴望让当下生活的混乱减少，换成更刚性的秩序。斯洛博迪安与塔诺夫写道：“说马斯克主义值得严肃对待，并不意味着它的成功是注定的。”但他们警告，二十一世纪的“制度性解体”可能意味着，马斯克主义将深刻塑造未来世界。

paragraphs.en: Spawning Season, by Joseph Osmundson (Bloomsbury). Anchored by a poignant account of his efforts to become a father, this memoir, by a microbiologist, is also a wide-ranging meditation on what it means to be biologically “fit” in Western society today. Though the barriers to parenthood which Osmundson encounters don’t exist for all of us (he’s queer; he’s male; he’s not able to afford surrogacy or adoption), his emotional journey will be instantly recognizable to anyone whose dreams of having a child have been dashed or deferred. Some of that arc is grappling with various reasons not to have a child, including climate change. A secondary narrative, following salmon on their journey to reproduce in warming waters, proves surprisingly apposite, partly thanks to Osmundson’s expertise on the subject, and partly because it underscores what is uniquely human about his dilemma: that, for us, a universal instinct can also be a question.
paragraphs.zh: 《Spawning Season》（作者：Joseph Osmundson，Bloomsbury 出版社）。这本回忆录由一位微生物学家撰写，以其努力成为父亲的真挚叙述为核心，也是一场关于当代西方社会中“生物学意义上的合格”到底意味着什么的广阔思考。虽然 Osmundson 遇到的生育障碍并不适用于我们所有人（他是酷儿；他是男性；他负担不起代孕或收养），但他的情感历程将立刻让每个梦想生育被打断或延后的人产生共鸣。他的叙事弧线中有一部分是在与多种“不想要孩子”的理由作斗争，其中包括气候变化。一个并行叙事，追踪鲑鱼在变暖水域中的繁殖之旅，出人意料地贴切，一方面得益于 Osmundson 在这一主题上的专长，另一方面也因为它凸显了他困境中独属于人类的一点：对我们而言，一个看似普遍的本能也可以变成一个问题。
